purpose: Follow this specific repository's test file naming, directory structure, and helper-reuse conventions so new tests fit the existing suite.
useWhen: Any test file added or modified in this repository (codex-plugin-cc)
dontUseWhen: Working in a different repository with its own test conventions
requiredInputs: an existing sibling test file to match style against
procedure: Name every test file `*.test.mjs` and place it directly under `tests/` (no nested subdirectories)
Reuse `tests/helpers.mjs` for shared setup (temp working dirs, PATH shims for the fake `codex` binary) instead of duplicating that logic
Use `tests/fake-codex-fixture.mjs` whenever a test needs to fake the `codex` binary or the app-server JSONL protocol, rather than hand-rolling a stub
Match the existing style: `node:test`'s `test()`/`describe()`, `node:assert/strict` assertions, no external test framework
verificationSteps: Shared setup goes through `tests/helpers.mjs` rather than being duplicated inline
`npm test` (which runs `node --test tests/*.test.mjs`) picks up and passes the new file automatically
doneWhen: The new test file follows the naming and helper-reuse conventions above and passes under `npm test`
escalateWhen: The existing helpers/fixtures do not support the scenario being tested and extending them is outside the task's declared scope
